jokes.text: Yeah, until you're defrosted and wake up refreshed, Unaware of any time having passed.
jokes.setup: Character explaining prison sentence as if it's a positive experience
jokes.punchline: Completely defeats the purpose of punishment by making it sound like a spa treatment
jokes.explanation: Solid ironic observation about futuristic punishment systems, well-delivered but not exceptional

jokes.text: Oh, please. It's probably just the carbonite thawing slightly.
jokes.setup: Someone worried the frozen prisoner is still conscious
jokes.punchline: Dismissive technical explanation that misses the horrifying implication
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan delivery of casual dismissal of something disturbing

jokes.text: While I stay here and get hooted at. (whirring) Ooh, yeah, baby! Oh, stop.
jokes.setup: Guard mentions getting hooted at by prisoners
jokes.punchline: Immediate catcalling followed by mock embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Quick visual gag with good timing, predictable but effective

jokes.text: That's silicon red, the universe's greatest folk singer!
jokes.setup: Seeing a famous person
jokes.punchline: Silicon Red - plays on folk music being organic while this is a silicon-based being
jokes.explanation: Clever name that plays on the contradiction between folk music's earthiness and sci-fi setting

jokes.text: Ah, still cocked.
jokes.setup: Getting gun back from prison property
jokes.punchline: Casually noting weapon is ready to fire as if checking the time
jokes.explanation: Perfect casual delivery of something that should be concerning

jokes.text: Have you forgotten My lifelong dream of being a folk singer... Because I sure have until right now.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming this was always his dream
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits he just made it up
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - the misdirection and honesty about his own BS is expertly crafted

jokes.text: Hey, I have a lifelong dream too. Of not being left alone with a frozen supervillain.
jokes.setup: Fry also has a lifelong dream
jokes.punchline: His dream is just basic survival in this specific moment
jokes.explanation: Good contrast to Bender's grandiose fake dream with Fry's immediate practical concern

jokes.text: Don't try anything, dr. Brutaloff! I know karate. Aye-hyah!
jokes.setup: Fry trying to intimidate a supervillain
jokes.punchline: Pathetic karate demonstration
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy bit, fits character but not particularly original

jokes.text: Then I shot him and stabbed him and stole all his gold / and I tried on his face while his body turned cold
jokes.setup: Folk song about violence and theft
jokes.punchline: Escalates to wearing someone's face like a mask
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that parodies folk music's casual violence while going grotesquely further

jokes.text: Old salmonella? Son, I ought to kill you for asking.
jokes.setup: Bender asking about the guitar
jokes.punchline: Guitar named after a disease, violent overreaction to simple question
jokes.explanation: Great character-specific humor - naming guitar after bacteria and threatening violence over minor request

jokes.text: 30? You must be quite a bungling lowlife.
jokes.setup: Silicon Red mentioning 30 convictions
jokes.punchline: Bender insulting someone for being bad at crime
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - critiquing someone's criminal competence

jokes.text: You're the sucker, sucker. See... With all salmonella and me been through, her sound is unique.
jokes.setup: Silicon Red claiming Bender is the sucker
jokes.punchline: Guitar's uniqueness comes from shared experiences with a disease-named instrument
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the technology vs. authenticity theme

jokes.text: Using my fancy technology, I can make an exact copy of this guitar.
jokes.setup: Silicon Red's claim technology can't copy it
jokes.punchline: Immediate confident contradiction
jokes.explanation: Good immediate contradiction timing

jokes.text: I call the process 'science.'
jokes.setup: Explaining complex technological process
jokes.punchline: Reducing it to the most basic term possible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant deadpan delivery - the ultimate reductive explanation with perfect economy

jokes.text: Whoa, that's a lot of porn.
jokes.setup: Looking through Bender's files for guitar photo
jokes.punchline: Discovering extensive porn collection
jokes.explanation: Predictable but fits Bender's character perfectly

jokes.text: I don't know. I mainly hang out in here.
jokes.setup: Where did you store the guitar picture
jokes.punchline: Bender admitting he spends most of his time in the porn folder
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender honesty about his priorities

jokes.text: Witchcraft! Sorcerer!
jokes.setup: Seeing 3D printing technology
jokes.punchline: Folk singer reacting like a medieval peasant
jokes.explanation: Good character-appropriate reaction to technology

jokes.text: Shall we adjourn to the porn folder?
jokes.setup: Having time to kill while waiting
jokes.punchline: Suggesting they browse Bender's porn collection together
jokes.explanation: Callbacks the porn folder with appropriately awkward suggestion

jokes.text: I call my new guitar bender mae because bender may be The greatest folk singer of all time!
jokes.setup: Naming his new guitar
jokes.punchline: Terrible pun on his own name while proclaiming his greatness
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender ego combined with terrible pun

jokes.text: I tell people she got it while I was doing hard time With a new hampshire chain gang.
jokes.setup: Explaining fake authenticity of guitar scratch
jokes.punchline: New Hampshire chain gang - impossibly soft version of hard time
jokes.explanation: Great detail - New Hampshire as least intimidating place for chain gang

jokes.text: From where dr. Brutaloff slashed me with his finger knives, Right after he thawed out of his carbonite, And right before he froze me in his carbonite Because you deserted me.
jokes.setup: Fry also has a scratch
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dramatic and accusatory backstory
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and callback to earlier abandonment

jokes.text: Eh, you're always getting frozen in stuff. It's your thing, man.
jokes.setup: Fry's dramatic tale of being frozen
jokes.punchline: Dismissive observation about Fry's recurring fate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender dismissiveness and meta-commentary on Fry's recurring situations

jokes.text: I claim I won it in a knife fight at j. Crew.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he got his flannel shirt
jokes.punchline: Knife fight at the least threatening store possible
jokes.explanation: Brilliant juxtaposition of violent backstory with preppy retail store - perfectly economical and quotable

jokes.text: Actual folk songs are all public domain. Where's the money in that?
jokes.setup: Question about knowing actual folk songs
jokes.punchline: Purely mercenary view of musical authenticity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender - reducing artistic tradition to profit potential

jokes.text: But, robot, you can't just make up folk songs Like you can a medical diploma.
jokes.setup: Explaining you can't fake folk songs
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of having fake medical credentials
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg - accidentally revealing something horrible while making a point

jokes.text: 36% of all folk-song heroes work on a railroad And are named 'big' something.
jokes.setup: Analyzing folk music patterns
jokes.punchline: Reducing romantic tradition to statistics
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about folk music tropes

jokes.text: 75% of these big railroad men have bad-hearted women Who done them wrong, Usually with a smooth-talking rambler.
jokes.setup: Continuing folk music analysis
jokes.punchline: More statistical reduction of romantic tragedy
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern analysis theme

jokes.text: Somebody kills somebody, blah, blah, blah. / somebody kills somebody, blah, blah, blah
jokes.setup: Summarizing folk music endings
jokes.punchline: Reducing dramatic conclusions to 'blah blah blah' then accidentally singing it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation where Bender accidentally creates what he's mocking

jokes.text: Man, all this corncob pipe smoke Is irritating my stab wounds.
jokes.setup: Complaining about smoke
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of stab wounds as the problem
jokes.explanation: Good casual mention of violence as mundane concern

jokes.text: I'm ramblin' rodriguez, the new greatest folk singer ever.
jokes.setup: Introducing himself on stage
jokes.punchline: Immediately declaring himself the greatest ever
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender ego on display

jokes.text: 'cause your song was insincere, boy.
jokes.setup: Bender asking why he was booed
jokes.punchline: Direct assessment of his artificial approach to folk music
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective critique that drives the plot

jokes.text: And don't go to prison. That's my shtick!
jokes.setup: Telling Bender to get life experience
jokes.punchline: Protective of his criminal brand
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - treating criminal history as intellectual property

jokes.text: How can I be so bad at everything I try And still be so great?
jokes.setup: Bender reflecting on his failure
jokes.punchline: Inability to comprehend that he's not actually great
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender self-delusion - the contradiction is the entire joke

jokes.text: The heartless robot is bender. The best friend is me. How can you people be so blind?
jokes.setup: Fry trying to explain his metaphor
jokes.punchline: Having to spell out his own victimization
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment of having to explain obvious metaphor

jokes.text: Just as I now understand the ape-men of earth.
jokes.setup: Explaining how Bender should understand railroad workers
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to humans as ape-men
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg casual insult to humans

jokes.text: Uh, I'm looking for the wrong side of the tracks.
jokes.setup: Bender trying to find rough area
jokes.punchline: Literal interpretation of the phrase
jokes.explanation: Simple literal interpretation joke

jokes.text: Then you've come to the right side of the tracks.
jokes.setup: Bender's literal interpretation
jokes.punchline: Continuing the literal wordplay
jokes.explanation: Continues the literal interpretation but predictably

jokes.text: Hammer, that should be easy to rhyme. You done time in the slammer? Uh, nope. Been to alabama? Sorry. How's your grammar? Praiseworthy.
jokes.setup: Bender trying to find rhymes for 'hammer'
jokes.punchline: Increasingly desperate attempts, ending with formal grammar assessment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from criminal past to grammar skills

jokes.text: I was got drunk and ate my big toe. Can't say I miss it.
jokes.setup: Hobo sharing his story
jokes.punchline: Casual attitude toward self-cannibalism
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny casual admission with good deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Jiggers! I dropped my sexton in the stew.
jokes.setup: Cookie the inept surveyor
jokes.punchline: Navigation instrument in the food
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific incompetence

jokes.text: And it's the same railroad camp. I mean, the irony is palpable.
jokes.setup: Having to deliver dynamite to railroad camp
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic assessment of mild coincidence
jokes.explanation: Mild irony joke with overwrought delivery

jokes.text: Dibs on the toes.
jokes.setup: Explosion aftermath
jokes.punchline: Claiming body parts as salvage
jokes.explanation: Darkly economical - two words that imply a lot

jokes.text: But, bender, I never spied no bad-hearted woman.
jokes.setup: Bender writing song about him
jokes.punchline: Protesting the fictional narrative
jokes.explanation: Sets up the reality vs. song narrative theme

jokes.text: Wow, creativity's hard work. (slurps loudly)
jokes.setup: Making up story details
jokes.punchline: Treating invention as physical labor while drinking
jokes.explanation: Good Bender laziness disguised as artistic struggle

jokes.text: The part where it ended. (laughs)
jokes.setup: Bender asking about favorite part of song
jokes.punchline: Blunt criticism that it was too long
jokes.explanation: Good direct insult with economy

jokes.text: You've accurately portrayed the nature of my grievance.
jokes.setup: Hearing himself depicted in Bender's song
jokes.punchline: Formal acknowledgment of accurate betrayal portrayal
jokes.explanation: Good formal language for petty complaint

jokes.text: Oh, good, I won't have To change any words.
jokes.setup: Learning the woman's name is actually Jezebel
jokes.punchline: Relief at not having to revise his song
jokes.explanation: Good Bender laziness - prioritizing convenience over morality

jokes.text: Oh, my god, reality is infringing bender's copyright.
jokes.setup: They realize Bender's song is coming true
jokes.punchline: Fry frames it as a copyright infringement rather than magic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant inversion - treating magical realism as intellectual property law. Perfect Futurama absurdist logic.

jokes.text: Oh, my god, reality is infringing bender's copyright.
jokes.setup: Realizing life is copying Bender's song
jokes.punchline: Treating reality as intellectual property violation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist reversal - reality violating fiction's rights rather than the reverse

jokes.text: I don't think so, bender. You deserted me once, and now it's your turn to 'feel the chill.' Ha!
jokes.setup: Bender asking Fry for help
jokes.punchline: Fry using the exact song lyric to reject him
jokes.explanation: Satisfying callback with Fry getting to use Bender's own lyrics against him.

jokes.text: I don't care if it's three b.M. Open up and hide me!
jokes.setup: Fry saying it's 3 AM
jokes.punchline: Bender mishearing/misspeaking as 3 BM
jokes.explanation: Weak bathroom humor pun

jokes.text: That doesn't sound like me. Fry? But you just-- eh, I'll figure it out later.
jokes.setup: Fry claims the Professor saw him refuse to help Bender
jokes.punchline: Professor denies it, creating confusion that he gives up on immediately
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor moment - faces paradox and immediately loses interest. Shows his character perfectly.

jokes.text: We got a 20 on the tin man. Toodle-oo.
jokes.setup: Using CB radio
jokes.punchline: Mixing trucker jargon with polite goodbye
jokes.explanation: Good character-appropriate mixing of registers

jokes.text: He's a unique and beautiful object.
jokes.setup: Discussing whether Fry could be copied
jokes.punchline: Using the exact phrase from earlier about the guitar
jokes.explanation: Good callback that applies earlier phrase to Fry

jokes.text: I believe the correct plural is octopi.
jokes.setup: Amy saying 'octopuses'
jokes.punchline: Pedantic correction in midst of crisis
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - academic pedantry during emergency

jokes.text: I'm sorry. 'octopuses' is also acceptable.
jokes.setup: Just corrected the grammar
jokes.punchline: Immediately backing down while still being attacked
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of pedantic character during crisis

jokes.text: Think about puppies, bender, Cute, harmless, dead puppies.
jokes.setup: Trying to get Bender to think of safe things
jokes.punchline: Accidentally making puppies sinister
jokes.explanation: Good dark escalation where trying to help makes it worse

jokes.text: What kind of lazy ending is that? I'm not gonna put that in my song.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to create another octopus
jokes.punchline: Bender critiquing lazy writing while in a crisis
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on writing quality

jokes.text: My audience deserves better than some crappy, formulaic ending.
jokes.setup: Refusing to use simple solution
jokes.punchline: Sudden artistic integrity when his life is at stake
jokes.explanation: Good character development - Bender showing unexpected principles

jokes.text: If I must die, Let me die in a blaze of artistic integrity.
jokes.setup: Refusing to compromise artistic vision
jokes.punchline: Dramatically choosing death over bad writing
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of unexpected Bender nobility

jokes.text: crushed him flatter than a macbook air, lord, lord / crushed him flatter than a macbook air.
jokes.setup: Traditional folk song about being crushed
jokes.punchline: Modern technology reference in old-timey song
jokes.explanation: Good anachronistic humor mixing folk tradition with tech reference

jokes.text: But you said making a copy of yourself Would be a lousy ending.
jokes.setup: Bender used the copy solution he rejected
jokes.punchline: Pointing out his contradiction
jokes.explanation: Points out logical inconsistency

jokes.text: No, duplicate me said that.
jokes.setup: Amy pointing out his contradiction
jokes.punchline: Blaming his duplicate for having integrity
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bender logic - disowning his own principles by attributing them to his duplicate. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: See, I had to be sure he wouldn't try And weasel out of getting killed Like I would and did, So I created him with a tragic weakness: Artistic integrity.
jokes.setup: Explaining his duplicate strategy
jokes.punchline: Treating artistic integrity as a fatal flaw he deliberately programmed in
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Bender treating principles as a weakness to exploit. The casual admission of cowardice while praising his own cleverness is perfect.

jokes.text: So I created him with a tragic weakness: Artistic integrity.
jokes.setup: Explaining how he outsmarted his duplicate
jokes.punchline: Treats artistic integrity as a fatal flaw
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender worldview - artistic integrity as weakness rather than strength. Brilliant character consistency.

jokes.text: How could we have thought a guy with integrity Was you even for a second?
jokes.setup: Bender's explanation of the duplicate's weakness
jokes.punchline: Realizing integrity was the obvious tell that it wasn't really Bender
jokes.explanation: Good followup that reinforces Bender's character consistency
